info.title: Sendtrap API
info.summary: HTTP API for Sendtrap inboxes — list, inspect, assert on and manage captured email.
info.description:
Sendtrap is an SMTP catcher / email sandbox. Every inbox exposes this HTTP API,
authenticated by the inbox's own API token. The API is served by both Sendtrap
Community (self-hosted) and Sendtrap Cloud from the same core package; this
document is the authoritative contract for the `/api/v1` surface and the
Mailtrap-compatible alias routes.

## Authentication

Every request must carry the inbox API token, either as a bearer token:

```
Authorization: Bearer <api_token>
```

or in the `X-Api-Token` header. The token scopes the request to exactly one
inbox; there is no cross-inbox access. Find the token on the inbox's
Settings page, or in the `api_token` field of `GET /api/v1/inbox`.

Requests from IP addresses outside the inbox's configured allow-list are
rejected with `403` even when the token is valid.

## Rate limiting

Two limiters apply, keyed to the inbox (or to the caller IP when the token
is invalid):

- **General** (`inbox-api`): applies to every endpoint. The ceiling is
  host-configured (Community: `SENDTRAP_API_REQUESTS_PER_MINUTE`, unlimited
  by default with a 300/min fallback where a limit applies; Cloud: per plan).
- **Blocking** (`inbox-api-wait`, 15/min): additionally applies to
  `POST /v1/assert` and to `GET /v1/messages` when a `wait` parameter is
  given, because those requests can hold a server worker open.

Throttled responses include `X-RateLimit-Limit` / `X-RateLimit-Remaining`;
a rejected request returns `429` with `Retry-After`.

## Blocking waits

`GET /v1/messages?wait=N` and `POST /v1/assert` with `timeout: N` block
server-side until a matching message arrives or `N` seconds elapse. `N` is
clamped to a server-configured ceiling (default 30 s). An assertion that
times out is **not** an error: `/assert` always returns `200` with
`matched: false` when nothing arrived.

## Editions and gated features

The contract is identical in Community and Cloud. Where a feature is gated
by plan in Cloud, the operation carries an `x-sendtrap-availability`
extension: `community` describes availability in Sendtrap Community,
`cloud` in Sendtrap Cloud. Calling a gated endpoint without access
returns `403`. Currently gated: the HTML compatibility check API
(`GET /v1/messages/{id}/compatibility` and `min_compatibility_score` on
`/assert`) — always available in Community, paid plans in Cloud.

## Mailtrap compatibility

The `/sandboxes/{sandbox}/...` routes mirror a subset of the Mailtrap
sandbox API so an existing Mailtrap test helper works after swapping only
the base URL and token. The `{sandbox}` path segment is accepted but not
validated — the token already scopes the request to one inbox. Fields with
no Sendtrap equivalent (templates, blacklist reports, POP3, granular
permissions) are omitted rather than faked.

## Versioning

`/api/v1` is a compatibility surface: fields may be added, existing fields
and semantics are not changed or removed within v1. The authoritative
revision of this document is the one shipped in the `sendtrap/core` release
(`openapi/sendtrap.yaml`) matching your installed version.

servers.description: Your Sendtrap instance (Community self-hosted install, or Sendtrap Cloud).

baseUrl.description: Root URL of the Sendtrap instance, without a trailing slash.

tags.name: Inbox
tags.description: The authenticated inbox itself — credentials, limits, message counts.

tags.name: Messages
tags.description: List, read, download and manage captured messages.

tags.name: Testing
tags.description: Blocking assertions for CI / end-to-end test suites.

tags.name: Mailtrap compatibility
tags.description: Drop-in aliases for a subset of the Mailtrap sandbox API.

get.description:
Details of the inbox the API token belongs to, including its SMTP
credentials and the total message count. Because the caller has proven
possession of this inbox's own token, credential fields
(`smtp_username`, `smtp_password`, `api_token`, `auto_forward_to`,
`webhook_url`) are always included here.

Messages in the inbox, newest first, paginated with Laravel's standard
`data` / `links` / `meta` envelope.

With `wait` > 0 and an initially empty result, the request blocks until
at least one matching message arrives or `wait` seconds (clamped to the
server ceiling, default 30) elapse — useful in tests to await mail
without polling. Blocking requests count against the tighter
`inbox-api-wait` limiter (15/min). A timeout is not an error: the
response is `200` with an empty `data` array.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Substring match against subject, from address and from name.

parameters.name: subject_contains
parameters.description: Substring match against the subject only.

parameters.name: test_id
parameters.description: Exact match against the message's `X-Sendtrap-Test-Id` header value.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description:
Substring match against recipients — both the To/Cc headers and the
SMTP envelope (so BCC-only recipients match too).

parameters.name: wait
parameters.description:
Maximum seconds to block waiting for a first matching message.
`0` (default) returns immediately. Clamped server-side
(default ceiling 30 s).

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Page size. No server-side maximum is currently enforced.

delete.description:
Deletes messages, including stored raw sources and attachments. With no
filters, deletes **every** message in the inbox. With filters (same
semantics as the list endpoint), deletes only the matching messages —
e.g. `?test_id=run-42` lets a test run on a shared inbox clean up just
its own mail.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Substring match against subject, from address and from name.

parameters.name: subject_contains
parameters.description: Substring match against the subject only.

parameters.name: test_id
parameters.description: Exact match against the message's `X-Sendtrap-Test-Id` header value.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Substring match against To/Cc headers and the SMTP envelope.

post.summary: Assert that a matching message arrives
post.description:
The primary testing endpoint: waits (up to `timeout` seconds, clamped
to the server ceiling, default 30) for a message matching every given
filter, then returns `200` with `matched: true/false` and the matched
message, if any. A non-match is an expected assertion outcome, not an
HTTP error — check the `matched` flag.

Filters combine with AND. The newest matching message wins. With
`min_compatibility_score`, a candidate only matches once its HTML
compatibility ratio meets the threshold (see the HTML Check tag for
availability).

Rate-limited by the `inbox-api-wait` limiter (15/min) regardless of
timeout value.

to.description: Substring match against To/Cc headers and the SMTP envelope.

search.description: Substring match against subject, from address and from name.

test_id.description: Exact match against the `X-Sendtrap-Test-Id` header value.

timeout.description: Maximum seconds to wait. `0` checks once and returns immediately.

min_compatibility_score.description:
Minimum HTML compatibility ratio (0–100) the matched
message must reach. Requires HTML Check API access.

otp-mail.summary: Wait up to 10 s for a password-reset mail to alice

message.description: The matched message, or `null` when `matched` is `false`.

403.description:
IP not allowed, or `min_compatibility_score` used without HTML
Check API access.

get.description:
Full message detail: parsed bodies, headers in original order,
extracted links, quality checks, attachment metadata and download URLs.

patch.description: The only mutable field is `is_read`. Omitting it marks the message read.

get.description:
The HTML body with inline `cid:` references rewritten to attachment
URLs, ready for display. For a text-only message, returns the text
body wrapped in a `<pre>` block.

Email-client compatibility score for the message's HTML, with a
per-feature issue breakdown (based on caniemail reference data).
Computed on demand and cached per message.

403.description: IP not allowed, or the caller lacks HTML Check API access.

200.description: The attachment content, as a download.

Content-Disposition.description: Attachment disposition with the original filename.

get.description:
Returns a plain JSON array (no pagination envelope), 30 messages per
page, newest first. `last_id` takes precedence over `page` for
cursor-style iteration.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Substring match against subject, To header and from name.

parameters.name: last_id
parameters.description: Return only messages with an id lower than this (cursor pagination).

patch.summary: Update a message's read state (Mailtrap alias)
patch.description: Accepts either the Mailtrap-style nested body or a flat `is_read` field.

200.description: The text body (empty string when the message has none).

get.summary: Get the rendered HTML body (Mailtrap alias)
Get the original HTML source (Mailtrap alias)
get.description: The HTML part exactly as received, without `cid:` rewriting (empty string when absent).

get.summary: Get the raw RFC 822 source (Mailtrap alias)

200.description: The raw message as an attachment download.

get.description:
Headers as a single name→value object. When a header occurs more than
once (e.g. `Received`), only the last occurrence is kept — use
`GET /v1/messages/{messageId}` for the full ordered header list.

200.description: The attachment content, as a download.

Content-Disposition.description: Attachment disposition with the original filename.

200.description: The sandbox with updated unread count.

bearerToken.description: The inbox API token as a bearer token.

apiTokenHeader.name: X-Api-Token
apiTokenHeader.description: The inbox API token as a plain header (alternative to the bearer form).

messageId.name: messageId
messageId.description: Numeric message id. Messages belonging to a different inbox return `404`.

attachmentId.name: attachmentId
attachmentId.description: Numeric attachment id. Must belong to the addressed message, else `404`.

sandbox.name: sandbox
sandbox.description:
Accepted for Mailtrap URL compatibility but **not validated** — the API
token alone determines the inbox. Any value works; generated URLs use
the inbox's SMTP username.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Request ceiling per minute for the applicable limiter.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Requests remaining in the current window.

IpForbidden.description: The caller's IP address is outside the inbox's allow-list.

value.message: Access denied for your IP address.

NotFound.description: No such resource in this inbox (includes resources owned by other inboxes).

Retry-After.description: Seconds until the limiter window resets.

Error.description: Common error envelope for every non-2xx JSON response.

EmailAddress.description: A parsed mailbox from an address header.

MessageSummary.description: Lightweight message representation used in lists and assertion results.

test_id.description: Value of the `X-Sendtrap-Test-Id` header, if the sender set one.

has_unresolved_merge_tags.description: Whether unreplaced template tags like `{{name}}` were detected.

MessageDetail.description: Full message representation with parsed bodies, headers and attachments.

message_id.description: The Message-ID header value, without angle brackets.

unresolved_merge_tags.description: The unreplaced template tags found in the bodies, e.g. `{{first_name}}`.

html.description: Original HTML body (`null` when the message has no HTML part).

links.description: Deduplicated `href` targets from the HTML body (excluding `mailto:`/`tel:`/fragment-only anchors).

checks.description:
Message-quality lint results. An `html_compatibility` entry is
included only when a cached HTML Check result exists and the caller
has HTML Check API access.

headers.description: All header lines in original order (duplicates preserved).

key.description:
Check identifier. Current checks: `missing_text_part`,
`oversized_html`, `missing_list_unsubscribe`,
`from_address_present`, `html_compatibility`. New checks may be
added within v1.

is_inline.description: Whether the part is an inline (`cid:`-referenced) asset rather than a file attachment.

url.description: Authenticated download URL for this attachment.

Inbox.description:
The authenticated inbox. Credential fields are always present for
API-token callers (possession of the token is the credential).

smtp_host.description: Public SMTP host to point your application's mailer at.

auto_forward_to.description: Address real copies are forwarded to, when configured.

webhook_url.description: Webhook notified on message arrival, when configured.

allowed_ips.description: Inbox-level IP/CIDR allow-list ([] = no inbox-level restriction).

effective_allowed_ips.description: The allow-list actually enforced (inbox and instance/workspace levels combined).

messages_count.description: Total messages currently in the inbox.

compatibility_ratio.description:
Percentage (0–100, one decimal) of detected HTML/CSS features with
full support across the checked client set. `100.0` when no
checkable features were detected.

issues.description: One entry per HTML feature with incomplete client support, errors first.

severity.description: `error` when fewer than half of the checked clients support the feature.

note.description: Support caveat note, when the reference data has one.

html_body_size.description: HTML body size in bytes (0 when the message has no HTML part).

transfer_encoding.description: Always `null` (not tracked; present for Mailtrap shape compatibility).

MessagePage.description: Standard Laravel pagination envelope for message lists.
